name: GK United Credit Repair
description_short: GK United is a credit repair company claiming 100,000+ disputes won that helps clients remove inaccuracies from credit reports and rebuild credit scores through disputing with major bureaus.
description_long:
GK United LLC is a credit repair company based in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, operating since at least 2016 (claiming 25 years experience, though verification needed). The company is led by co-founders George Koufalis (10+ years experience), Eric Vilata (8+ years experience), and Carl Orner (10+ years experience). GK United positions itself as a specialized firm focused on disputing inaccurate negative items on credit reports with the three major credit bureaus. The company reports handling 10,523+ credit cases and claims 100,000 disputes won as of their website snapshot.

GK United offers credit repair services focused on disputing inaccuracies on credit reports, credit consulting and guidance on rebuilding credit, and a credit specialist certification program for individuals wanting to start their own credit repair business. They market a "Fixed Right Promise" and claim no upfront payments with a satisfaction guarantee. The company operates Monday through Saturday, 9am-6pm, and offers free quote consultations via phone, text, and web form.

The company differentiates itself through emphasis on longevity (claimed 25 years), team expertise depth (multiple specialists with 8-10+ years each), and their certification training program. They highlight having 251 certified specialists trained through their program and claim a satisfaction guarantee. Their website emphasizes they work with all three major credit bureaus and position expertise as a key selling point.

Key limitations: The website contains several red flags including outdated copyright (2026), broken About and Services pages returning 404 errors, unverified claims about 25-year history and dispute numbers, and generic marketing language. No specific details about pricing structure, dispute methodology, timeline expectations, or regulatory compliance appear on accessible pages. The claims about "100,000 disputes won" lack third-party verification. The company's exact legal standing, licensing, and CFPB complaint history are not addressed on the website. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Program
features: Credit report analysis across all three bureaus
Dispute filing for inaccurate and unverifiable items

pros: Claims no upfront payment policy, addressing primary credit repair scam concern
Offers free quote consultation before commitment
Multiple team members with 8-10+ years stated experience in credit repair
Operates in-house specialist certification program, suggesting internal expertise development
Provides both dispute services and credit consulting guidance
Extended business hours (Mon-Sat, 9am-6pm) with multiple contact methods (phone, text, web)
cons: Critical website functionality broken (About and Services pages return 404 errors), limiting transparency
Claims like '25 years experience' and '100,000 disputes won' lack independent verification or third-party sources
No pricing information, fee structure, or payment terms disclosed on accessible website
No mention of CFPB complaints, regulatory compliance, licensing, or dispute resolution processes
Generic marketing language without specific methodology details, timeline expectations, or documented results
best_for: Consumers with documented inaccuracies on credit reports who want professional dispute assistance
Individuals seeking credit rebuilding guidance alongside dispute services
Those who prefer no upfront payment credit repair services
diagnosis: GK United targets consumers with credit report inaccuracies seeking professional dispute help without upfront costs. The main caveat is that critical website information is missing (broken pages, no pricing/methodology details), and major claims lack independent verification—customers should request detailed written service agreements, fees, timeline expectations, and verify the company's regulatory standing before engaging. Consumers comparing credit repair companies should also consider whether credit monitoring services, secured credit cards, or credit counseling might address their needs alongside or instead of paid credit repair services.
services: Credit report dispute processing with major credit bureaus
Credit consulting and financial health guidance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
